arguments.task: Open official Pipedrive developer and pricing/authentication documentation. Read and extract: one-line product/API description, all documented API auth methods, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, pricing or plan requirements for API access, and whether Pipedrive documents an MCP server. Prefer canonical first-party pages that load fully in the browser. Return the exact page URLs you used and concise notes tied to each URL.

input.thought: Start a focused browser run on first-party Pipedrive sources to gather canonical evidence.

raw_result.description: Sales CRM platform with a developer API and app marketplace for extending customer and deal workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Pipedrive Developer Hub states apps authenticate users with OAuth. The public API reference documents authentication via an api_token query parameter or Authorization header bearer token for requests. No first-party evidence was gathered here for API keys or HTTP Basic.
raw_result.gating_reason: The developer site indicates API access depends on a Pipedrive account/app context, but the gathered evidence in this pass did not capture a pricing page or explicit free-plan API entitlement. Treated as paid-plan-required based on product/account usage context and marked for review.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer documentation references a broad CRM API covering core sales objects and related account data through documented endpoints.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party MCP server documentation was confirmed in the gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Some structured fields were not fully verified from successfully captured canonical page text in this pass, especially explicit pricing/access gating and MCP absence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit details were not successfully captured from page text in this pass.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official Developer Hub and API v1 reference are publicly available on first-party domains, indicating a mature developer surface for CRM integrations.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The browser run confirmed official developer/API pages exist, but this pass did not successfully extract enough page text to fully support all structured decisions, especially access_tier, rate_limit_notes, and MCP absence. Uncertain structured fields were set conservatively for review.
